evidence_quotes.quote: make it unusable, even though many of the software have individual player components that do not require internet access or could also be hosted on private servers.

paragraphs.text: Please also use the Digital Fairness Act to proactively stop the destruction of digital games. A growing number of large publishers are moving towards “creating” games bought by consumers – i.e.: make it unusable, even though many of the software have individual player components that do not require internet access or could also be hosted on private servers. Keep up the situation in the same way: The owner of a tennis club, casts his members’ beatings and balls because he wants to rehabilitate the place. But next year they are welcome to play on the tennis square 2.0. Provided that they buy new beds and balls from him...
